skill.name: typeset
skill.description: Improve typography by fixing font choices, hierarchy, sizing, weight consistency, and readability.
skill.summary: Improve interface typography with clearer font choices, readable scale, hierarchy, and consistent weights.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found markdown prose and inline CSS examples, with no executable code, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False positive: inline markdown code markers
low_findings.description: The external command findings are markdown backticks around CSS terms and design examples. They do not execute shell, Ruby, or other commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain prose and inline CSS terms only. No command runner, script block, subprocess call, or user input execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptography patterns in prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are on descriptive text and a markdown reference. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage appears on these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss typography guidance and skill references. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, insecure random generation, or credential processing.

low_findings.title: False positive: system reconnaissance patterns in CSS guidance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are CSS sizing guidance for rem units and clamp usage. They do not inspect the host, environment, files, users, or network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are typography layout recommendations. No filesystem discovery, OS probing, environment inspection, or network enumeration is present.

content.value_statement: Generic typography makes interfaces harder to scan and read. This skill guides font selection, hierarchy, spacing, and accessibility checks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews font choices for brand fit, default usage, and excessive family counts.
Plans a consistent type scale for captions, body text, subheadings, and headings.
Improves readability through line length, line height, contrast, and minimum body size checks.
Defines consistent font weight roles for body text, labels, headings, and data-heavy views.
Recommends web font loading practices that reduce layout shift and avoid unused weights.
Verifies hierarchy, consistency, personality, performance, and WCAG-oriented accessibility concerns.
limitations: It provides design guidance and implementation direction, not an automated typography rendering engine.
It expects existing design context and references a frontend-design skill that is not packaged here.
It does not include scripts, tests, font files, or reusable design tokens.
Final font choices still require visual review in the target product or page.

use_cases.title: Audit Product Typography
use_cases.description: Review an existing interface for weak hierarchy, inconsistent weights, cramped text, and unreadable sizing.

use_cases.title: Refine Frontend Type Styles
use_cases.description: Convert scattered font sizes and weights into a consistent scale with readable defaults.

use_cases.title: Improve Content Page Readability
use_cases.description: Tune headings, body copy, line lengths, and responsive sizing for marketing or editorial pages.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Basic Typography
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a screen or page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the typography in this interface. Identify problems with font choice, hierarchy, sizing, line height, and readability.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Type Scale
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a consistent hierarchy for an existing UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a practical type scale for this product. Include roles for caption, secondary text, body, subheading, and heading.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Readability
prompt_templates.scenario: Refine dense screens or long content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve readability for this layout. Focus on line length, line height, body size, contrast, and text container width.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign Typography System
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign the typography system for this interface. Define font pairings, scale, weights, spacing rules, accessibility checks, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: A SaaS dashboard uses several close font sizes and weak heading contrast.
output: The audit identifies muddy hierarchy and inconsistent label weights.
The plan uses five clear type roles and stronger heading contrast.
The verification checks scanning speed, consistency, and readability at common viewport sizes.

output_examples.input: A marketing page has oversized body text, cramped headings, and long paragraphs.
output: The recommendation separates display headings from fixed body text.
Paragraph width is reduced for comfortable reading.
Line height and spacing are adjusted to support longer reading sessions.

output_examples.input: A design system has arbitrary font sizes and unclear weight usage.
output: The output defines named text roles instead of value-based names.
Weights are mapped to body, label, and heading responsibilities.
Unused font weights are removed from the loading plan.

best_practices: Start with the reading goal and interface density before changing fonts.
Use a small type scale with clear roles and consistent weight rules.
Verify typography with real content, responsive widths, and accessibility checks.
anti_patterns: Do not add many font families to make a design feel polished.
Do not use arbitrary sizes that differ by only one or two pixels.
Do not make body text smaller than readable defaults to fit more content.

faq.question: Does this skill choose specific fonts?
faq.answer: It can recommend font direction based on brand and context, but final selection needs visual review.

faq.question: Can it create CSS automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide implementation, but the skill itself contains no scripts or code generation tools.

faq.question: Is it useful for app interfaces?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives fixed scale guidance for dense product interfaces and dashboards.

faq.question: Is it useful for marketing pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports fluid heading guidance and fixed body text for content-heavy pages.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility checks?
faq.answer: Yes. It calls for readable body size, sufficient contrast, zoom support, and WCAG-oriented review.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide screenshots, target audience, brand direction, current styles, and the page or component goal.
